title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: First complete genome loaded onto a quantum computer
items.description: Researchers encode the tiny hepatitis D virus in an early step toward “quantum genomics”

items.title: Ten thousand years ago, human evolution went into overdrive
items.description: Ancient DNA reveals “massive” genetic shifts tied to rise of farming, wheels, and metal tools

items.title: Astronomers complete largest 3D map of the universe ever made
items.description: Five-year quest to chart 47 million galaxies yields insights into mysterious “dark energy”

items.title: ‘Invisible’ birds spotted with thermal imaging
items.description: Approach could help reveal which migrating species are most vulnerable to wind turbines and light pollution

items.title: Fog is a vital water resource. Could it disappear in a warming world?
items.description: California program promises serious attention to an “underdog” area of research

items.title: An interspecies grooming ritual may have been spotted in desert ants
items.description: First-of-its-kind observation suggests red harvester ants may look to smaller ants to pick off parasites

items.title: Rollout of powerful new HIV prevention tool in lower income countries gets a boost
items.description: Global Fund and U.S. government plan to make injectable lenacapavir available to 3 million people by 2028

items.title: Department of Energy’s tech incubator doubles down on fusion power
items.description: ARPA-E’s $135 million in new funds will match its total spending from previous 12 years

items.title: NSF names record number of graduate fellows, rebounding from 2025 dip
items.description: Distribution by field is also closer to the norm than last year’s class

items.title: Penguins become marine detectives, thanks to pollutant-detecting anklets
items.description: Scientific jewelry picks up “forever chemicals” that can harm wildlife and humans
